title: Smart collection schema generation with item schema inference
description:
When generating a schema from a collection entry with `pudl schema new --from <collection> --collection`, the app should:

1. For each item in the collection, run schema inference to find matching schemas
2. Group items by their inferred schema:
   - Items matching existing schemas (e.g., #Ec2Instance) → use those
   - Items with no match → generate new item schema(s)
3. Generate the collection schema as a union of all matched/generated item schemas:
   ```cue
   #Ec2InstanceCollection: [...(#Ec2Instance | #SomeOtherInstance)]
   ```

## Current Behavior
The --collection flag generates an object schema with schema_type: "collection" in metadata, but the schema is structurally an object, not an array. This is incorrect - collection schemas should be structurally arrays.

## Expected Behavior
- If all items match existing schemas → generate only the collection schema referencing them
- If some items don't match → generate new item schemas AND the collection schema
- Heterogeneous collections should produce union types: `[...(#TypeA | #TypeB)]`

## Example
```
pudl schema new --from govim-nupab --path aws/ec2:#Ec2InstanceCollection --collection
```

If govim-nupab contains 5 EC2 instances and #Ec2Instance already exists and matches all items:
```cue
#Ec2InstanceCollection: [...#Ec2Instance]
```

If 3 items match #Ec2Instance and 2 don't match anything:
```cue
#Ec2InstanceCollection: [...(#Ec2Instance | #Ec2InstanceVariant)]
```
(where #Ec2InstanceVariant is auto-generated for the unmatched items)

## Implementation Notes
- Use existing schema inference from internal/inference package
- Include all candidate schemas regardless of confidence level (revisit if needed)
- May need to generate multiple files if new item schemas are created
- Consider how to name auto-generated item schemas

title: Add 'pudl s' as alias for 'pudl schema'
description:
Add short command alias:
- 'pudl s' should work as shorthand for 'pudl schema'
- All subcommands should work: pudl s new, pudl s edit, pudl s reinfer, etc.
- All flags should work the same way

title: Add export functionality for data lake entries
description:
Add a 'pudl export' command that allows users to export data from the catalog to various formats (JSON, YAML, CSV, NDJSON) with filtering options.

Implementation:
1. Create cmd/export.go with exportCmd
2. Create internal/exporter/exporter.go with Exporter struct and ExportOptions
3. Support export modes:
   - Single entry: --id <entry-id>
   - By schema: --schema aws.#EC2Instance
   - By origin: --origin k8s-pods
   - By query: --query 'origin LIKE k8s%'
4. Output options:
   - --format: json, yaml, csv, ndjson (default: original format)
   - --output: file path (default: stdout)
   - --pretty: pretty-print JSON/YAML
5. Handle format conversion edge cases:
   - CSV flattening of nested JSON
   - YAML multi-document output
6. Add progress indicator for large exports

Natural complement to import functionality - enables data workflows.

Confidence: 85% | Effort: Medium | Impact: High

title: Add pagination support to list command
description:
Add --page and --per-page flags to the pudl list command for handling large catalogs. A TODO already exists in internal/lister/lister.go. Implementation involves:

1. Add pagination flags to cmd/list.go (--page, --per-page)
2. Update DisplayOptions struct in internal/lister/lister.go to include Page and PerPage fields
3. Calculate offset from page number: offset = (page - 1) * perPage
4. Update display to show pagination info: 'Showing 21-40 of 150 entries (page 2 of 8)'

Confidence: 95% | Effort: Low | Impact: Medium

title: Add stdin streaming support for pipe workflows
description:
Allow 'pudl import' to read from stdin, enabling pipe-based workflows like 'kubectl get pods -o json | pudl import -'.

Implementation:
1. Update runImportCommand() in cmd/import.go to detect stdin input:
   - Check if path is '-' or empty
   - Check os.Stdin.Stat() for piped data (ModeCharDevice == 0)
2. Create importFromStdin() function:
   - Read stdin to temp file (required for streaming parser compatibility)
   - Use --format flag for explicit format (required since no file extension)
   - Fall back to content-based format detection
3. Add --format flag to import command: 'json', 'yaml', 'csv', 'ndjson'
4. Clean up temp file after import
5. Generate origin from 'stdin' or allow --origin override

Example workflows:
- kubectl get pods -o json | pudl import - --format json --origin k8s-pods
- aws ec2 describe-instances | pudl import -
- cat data.json | pudl import --path -

Confidence: 87% | Effort: Low | Impact: Medium

title: Add goreleaser configuration for releases
description:
Add .goreleaser.yaml for automated releases with cross-compilation, checksums, and changelog generation.

Implementation:
1. Create .goreleaser.yaml with builds for linux/darwin on amd64/arm64
2. Configure ldflags to inject version, commit, and date into cmd.version/commit/date
3. Set up archives with tar.gz format including README and docs
4. Add checksum generation and changelog filtering
5. Update Makefile with 'release' and 'snapshot' targets
6. Note: CGO_ENABLED=1 required for SQLite - may need special handling for cross-compilation

Confidence: 93% | Effort: Low | Impact: High

title: Add pudl schema reinfer command to re-run schema inference on existing data
description:
Currently there is no way to re-run schema inference on data that has already been imported. This is needed when:

- New schemas have been added and you want existing data to match against them
- Schema cascade priorities have been modified
- You want to batch-update schema assignments without going through interactive review

**Proposed command:** \Manage CUE schemas used for data validation and organization in PUDL.

Schemas are organized by packages (aws, k8s, unknown, etc.) and stored in the
schema repository at ~/.pudl/schema/. Each schema is a CUE file that defines
the structure and validation rules for imported data.

Available subcommands:
- list:   Show available schemas organized by package
- add:    Add a new schema file to the repository
- status: Show uncommitted changes in the schema repository
- commit: Commit schema changes to version control
- log:    Show commit history of schema changes

Examples:
    pudl schema list                           # List all schemas
    pudl schema list --package aws             # List schemas in aws package
    pudl schema add aws.rds-instance my.cue    # Add new schema to aws package
    pudl schema status                         # Show uncommitted changes
    pudl schema commit -m "Add RDS schema"     # Commit changes
    pudl schema log                            # Show recent commits

Usage:
  pudl schema [flags]
  pudl schema [command]

Available Commands:
  add         Add a new schema to the repository
  commit      Commit schema changes to version control
  edit        Open a schema file in your editor
  list        List available schemas
  log         Show commit history of schema changes
  new         Generate a new schema from imported data
  review      Interactively review and improve schema assignments
  status      Show uncommitted changes in the schema repository

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for schema

Use "pudl schema [command] --help" for more information about a command.

**Flags:**
- \ - Re-infer schemas for all catalog entries
- \ - Re-infer schema for a specific entry
- \ - Re-infer only entries currently assigned to this schema (e.g., \)
- \ - Re-infer only entries from a specific origin
- \ - Show what would change without applying updates
- \ - Apply changes without confirmation prompt

**Example usage:**
\\\

**Implementation notes:**
- Use the existing \ from \
- Use \ from \ to update entries
- Filter logic can be borrowed from \
- Should show a summary of changes (X entries updated, Y unchanged, Z failed)

**Related commands:**
- \🔍 Schema Review Workflow
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
🚀 Starting new schema review session...

📊 Review Preview:
  Total items matching criteria: 6
  Items to review (max): 6

📋 Schema Breakdown:
  pudl.schemas/pudl/unknown@v0:#CatchAll: 5 items
  pudl.schemas/collections/collections:#Collection: 1 items

Proceed with review? [y/N]: Review cancelled. - Interactive version (already exists)
- \ - Validates against current schema (already exists)

title: Enhance tab completion for schemas, IDs, and origins
description:
Enhance the existing shell completion in cmd/completion.go to include dynamic completion for schema names, entry IDs, and origin names.

Implementation:
1. Implement completeSchemaNames() - load schemas from schema manager, filter by prefix
2. Implement completeEntryIDs() - query recent entries from database, show with description (origin, schema)
3. Implement completeOrigins() - get distinct origins from database via new GetDistinctOrigins() method
4. Register completion functions for relevant flags:
   - showCmd: --id -> completeEntryIDs
   - listCmd: --schema -> completeSchemaNames, --origin -> completeOrigins
   - validateCmd: --schema -> completeSchemaNames
   - importCmd: --schema -> completeSchemaNames (already done)
5. Add GetDistinctOrigins() method to database/catalog.go

Note: Completions require database access which adds slight latency, but UX benefit outweighs this.

Confidence: 88% | Effort: Low | Impact: Medium

title: Add batch revalidation command
description:
Add 'pudl revalidate' command to re-validate existing catalog entries against current schemas. Useful after updating schemas to see which entries now fail validation.

Implementation:
1. Create cmd/revalidate.go with revalidateCmd
2. Support filtering options:
   - --all: revalidate all entries
   - --schema <schema>: only entries with specific schema
   - --id <id>: single entry
   - --origin <origin>: entries from specific origin
3. For each entry:
   - Load data from stored path
   - Validate against assigned schema using CascadeValidator
   - If schema assignment changes, update catalog entry
   - Track old vs new schema, pass/fail status
4. Display summary:
   - Total entries processed
   - Passed/failed counts
   - Schema changes (entries that moved to different schema)
   - Detailed errors for failed entries
5. Add --dry-run flag to preview changes without updating catalog
6. Add progress bar for large catalogs

Confidence: 80% | Effort: Medium | Impact: Medium

title: Add shell tab-completion for pudl CLI
description:
Implement robust tab-completion for pudl commands and arguments:

- Tab-complete command names (import, list, schema, show, etc.)
- Tab-complete subcommands (schema new, schema edit, schema reinfer, etc.)
- Tab-complete flag names (--entry, --path, --schema, etc.)
- Tab-complete flag values where applicable:
  - --entry: complete with proquint IDs from catalog
  - --schema: complete with available schema names
  - --format: complete with json, yaml, csv, ndjson
  - --path for schema commands: complete with package:#Definition paths
- Support bash, zsh, and fish shells
- Use cobra's built-in completion generation as foundation

title: Improve CUE error parsing for human-readable messages
description:
Parse CUE validation errors to provide human-readable, actionable error messages instead of raw CUE output. This is explicitly marked as TODO in internal/validator/cascade_validator.go.

Implementation:
1. Create internal/validator/cue_error_parser.go with CUEErrorParser struct
2. Define ParsedError struct: Path, Expected, Got, Constraint, Suggestion
3. Implement regex patterns for common CUE error formats:
   - 'field.path: conflicting values X and Y' -> type mismatch
   - 'field.path: incomplete value' -> missing required field
   - 'field.path: value X not allowed' -> constraint violation
4. Update extractValidationErrors() in cascade_validator.go to use parser
5. Generate human-readable suggestions for each error type
6. Add comprehensive tests for error pattern matching

CUE errors are notoriously cryptic - this dramatically improves user experience.

Confidence: 88% | Effort: Medium | Impact: High

title: Add helpful comments to schema metadata fields in pudl schema new output
description:
When a user creates a schema with `pudl schema new`, the resulting schema file should include comments explaining the valid values and/or purpose of the following pudl metadata fields:

**Fields to document:**
- `schema_type` - valid values and when to use each
- `resource_type` - format and purpose
- `cascade_priority` - how priority values work
- `cascade_fallback` - valid formats and examples  
- `compliance_level` - valid values and their implications

**Location:** The comments should appear inline in the generated `_pudl` metadata block in the CUE schema file.

**Files involved:**
- `internal/schemagen/generator.go` - `generateCUEContent` function (lines 238-263) writes the _pudl metadata block
- `internal/review/schema.cue.tmpl` - template used by schema review workflow

**Example output (illustrative):**
```cue
_pudl: {
    schema_type: "base"  // Valid values: "base", "collection", "enriched"
    resource_type: "ec2.instance"  // Format: <package>.<definition_name>
    cascade_priority: 100  // Higher values = higher priority (0-1000)
    cascade_fallback: ["pudl.unknown.#CatchAll"]  // Schemas to try if this one doesn't match
    identity_fields: ["InstanceId"]
    tracked_fields: ["State", "Tags"]
    compliance_level: "strict"  // Valid values: "strict", "warn", "permissive"
}
```

title: Add JSON output mode for all commands
description:
Add --output/-o flag to all commands for machine-readable output, enabling scripting and automation.

Implementation:
1. Add persistent flag to cmd/root.go: --output/-o with values 'text', 'json', 'yaml'
2. Create internal/ui/output.go with OutputWriter struct
3. Define structured output types for each command:
   - ListOutput: entries array, total_count, page info
   - ImportOutput: id, source_path, stored_path, format, schema, record_count, validation_pass
   - ShowOutput: full entry details
   - ValidateOutput: validation results, errors array
4. Update each command to use OutputWriter instead of fmt.Printf
5. Ensure JSON schemas are stable for scripting compatibility

Benefits:
- Enables jq processing: pudl list -o json | jq '.entries[].id'
- CI/CD integration
- Scripting workflows

Confidence: 90% | Effort: Medium | Impact: High

title: Add pudl doctor command for workspace diagnostics
description:
Create a diagnostic command that checks workspace health, identifies issues, and suggests fixes.

Checks to implement:
- Workspace structure integrity (directories exist, permissions correct)
- Database integrity (PRAGMA integrity_check, orphaned entries)
- Schema repository validation (CUE syntax, module.cue exists)
- Git repository status (initialized, clean state)
- Configuration validation (config.yaml valid)
- Orphaned files (files in data/ not in catalog)

Implementation:
1. Create cmd/doctor.go with HealthCheck struct and check functions
2. Create internal/doctor/checks.go with individual check implementations
3. Each check returns CheckResult with status (ok/warning/error), message, details, and suggested fix command
4. Display results with colored output (green checkmark, yellow warning, red X)

Confidence: 92% | Effort: Medium | Impact: High

title: Add support for compressed files (gzip, zstd)
description:
Add transparent support for importing gzip and zstd compressed files without requiring manual decompression.

Implementation:
1. Add DetectCompression() function in internal/importer/detection.go
   - Check file extension (.gz, .gzip, .zst, .zstd)
   - Also check magic bytes (gzip: 0x1f 0x8b, zstd: 0x28 0xb5 0x2f 0xfd)
2. Add WrapReader() function to return decompressing reader
3. Integrate into import pipeline before format detection
4. Add dependency: github.com/klauspost/compress/zstd (stdlib has gzip)
5. Store original compressed file in raw storage, decompress only for processing
6. Update format detection to work on decompressed content

Common use cases:
- Log files (often gzipped)
- Cloud exports (AWS, GCP often compress)
- Large datasets

Confidence: 90% | Effort: Low | Impact: High

title: Add comprehensive benchmarks to Makefile
description:
Add benchmark targets and performance regression testing to the Makefile for professional development practices.

Implementation:
1. Add benchmark targets to Makefile:
   - bench: run all benchmarks with configurable count and time
   - bench-cpu: CPU profiling with pprof
   - bench-mem: memory profiling with pprof
   - bench-save: save benchmark results to benchmarks/ directory with date
   - bench-compare: compare current vs baseline using benchstat
2. Add quality gate targets:
   - lint: run golangci-lint
   - test-race: run tests with race detector
   - coverage: generate coverage report
   - ci: run all quality checks
3. Create benchmark tests in key packages:
   - internal/streaming/benchmark_test.go: JSON/YAML/CSV processing
   - internal/database/benchmark_test.go: query performance
   - internal/inference/benchmark_test.go: schema inference
4. Create benchmarks/ directory for storing results

Benefits: catches performance regressions, documents expected performance, enables optimization work.

Confidence: 85% | Effort: Low | Impact: Medium
